page: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The configuration provides 32 vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system features 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server type does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. The processor features a large cache hierarchy, including 48 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a substantial 480 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced vector and matrix extensions such as AVX-512 and AMX, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography instructions like AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the E32s_v7 demonstrates strong memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks, reaching 181.75 GB/sec, while small cached block reads average 2352.2 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is in the top tier at 35.62 GB/sec, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong at 183.59 GB/sec. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 43.89 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the system achieves a strong text generation speed of 298.63 tokens/sec on small LLM models and a strong prompt processing speed of 186.24 tokens/sec on medium models. However, single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests fall within the average range.

The qualitative cost profile of the E32s_v7 is defined by its high memory density relative to compute resources. This makes it a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The server is designed for memory-intensive applications, database operations, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, featuring 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 server utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth at 181.75 GB/sec, top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.62 GB/sec, and strong static web serving throughput of 43.89 GB/sec. CPU compute and database operations perform in the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes this server configuration suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and medium-scale language model inference.

tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density, advanced matrix extensions, and strong large-block memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and strong large-block memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU, requiring external Premium Storage
Suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and medium LLM inference
